extra.message: Arbitrary Python code execution via eval(). User input from request.form['expression'] (line 116) is passed directly to eval() on line 120 with no sanitization. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code such as __import__('os').system('id').

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

extra.message: OS command injection via unsanitized user input. The address parameter from request.form['address'] (line 136) is concatenated directly into a shell command via rp('nslookup ' + address). rp references os.popen (imported on line 4). An attacker can inject arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters, e.g. 'example.com; cat /etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
CWE-77: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')

extra.message: XML External Entity (XXE) injection vulnerability. The XML parser is created with etree.XMLParser(no_network=False, dtd_validation=True) on line 157, explicitly enabling network access and DTD validation. User-supplied XML from request.form['xml'] (line 156) is parsed with etree.fromstring() on line 159 without disabling entity resolution. An attacker can read local files via <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'> or perform SSRF attacks.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) via Jinja2. User input from request.form['name'] (line 189) is embedded into a template string using Python string formatting on line 204, then the template is rendered with render_template_string() on line 205. An attacker can inject template expressions like {{config}} or {{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}} to achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Hardcoded secret key in application code. Flask secret key is hardcoded as 'Harry Potter And The Deathly Hallows' on line 15. This key should be loaded from environment variables or secure configuration, not embedded in source code.

Debug mode is explicitly enabled in production code (app.debug = True on line 208). This enables the Flask debugger, which exposes sensitive information and allows arbitrary code execution through the interactive console. Debug mode should never be enabled in production.

cwe: CWE-215: Information Exposure Through Debug Information
